title: Chapter 166 – Implementation (2)
content:
Those were his first words as the Radiant Demon Wing Leader to the Alliance Leader.

Sword Star had apparently recognized Jeong Yeon-shin's short-lived constitution, but that shock aside, Jeong Yeon-shin had never once imagined being embraced.

His mother had died giving birth to him. Perhaps if his teacher, the Fortress Lord, embraced him, it might be different. No—even that would be unbearably embarrassing.

'Is the Fortress Lord the only supreme master without strange habits?'

Sword Star's lofty status was no reason to suppress his displeasure. Jeong Yeon-shin's brow creased slightly.

Sword Star Hyeon So-baek's lips turned downward. His feelings played openly across his face.

It was clearly an expression of regret, yet nothing in his conduct suggested authority.

His slight smile carried a playful edge. He had the bearing of an eccentric master.

Self-contained and independent.

Jeong Yeon-shin grasped Sword Star's temperament in an instant.

He lived in his own world. A supreme master said to define an era.

He understood the depth of Jeong Yeon-shin's realm and constitution and did not hesitate to show sympathy.

Utterly free-spirited. The title Sword Star suited him far better than Alliance Leader.

Hyeon So-baek stroked his chin.

Jeong Yeon-shin repeated the word without thinking. It originated from a poem by Du Fu, referring to the rarity of living to seventy. Since prestigious families had risen in the world, the term was seldom used.

[TL Note- Du Fu was poet during Tang Dynasty (700AD)]

But something else mattered more. Sword Star's age was not publicly known.

Even as the Radiant Demon Wing Leader, this was his first time hearing it. He had only just learned.

The boy made no effort to hide his suspicion.
The prestigious old man smiled with his handsome, middle-aged face.

He discussed otherworldly matters in an elderly fashion. His words and actions betrayed transcendent internal energy cultivation.

Behind the seemingly senile exterior was the image of a swordsman in relentless pursuit of the ultimate.

He was completely obsessed with martial arts.

The Alliance's supreme authority spoke. His expression suggested that Jeong Yeon-shin's very existence was like watching a grandchild at play.

It was strange and chilling. The swordsman said to challenge heaven with his blade showed no interest in the dynamics of power.

Only martial arts.

He saw nothing but Jeong Yeon-shin's constitution and martial prowess. The relations between Desolate Fortress and the Martial Alliance seemed to concern him not at all.

It was goodwill that only Sword Star could offer. The great hero who had beheaded the Thirteen Evil Sects' leader among their elite masters to protect common people.

In other words, he was transcendent. Endless light flickered in his eyes as he took in all of Jeong Yeon-shin.
He clicked his tongue as if unable to bring himself to ask. Jeong Yeon-shin simply listened.

His endless words were terrifying. They carried both fascination and anticipation.

He analyzed Jeong Yeon-shin with sharper insight than the Law Enforcement Leader. It was the discernment of a master rare in the world.

'So there's another non-taciturn supreme master besides the Blood Flame Sect Leader.'

As the boy thought this in silence—

Fan Dragon Zhuge Xian was seeing this side of the Alliance Leader for the first time.

This was not the kind man who simply nodded through leadership meetings.

He hadn't known such brilliant eyes existed behind that demeanor.

'Though he shows no interest in governance.'

The Alliance's young martial artists would feel deep deprivation if they witnessed this.

Even his own heart felt strangely hollow. To think the first person Sword Star chose to discuss martial arts with was a Desolate Fortress master.

Having heard about the banquet hall incident earlier, Zhuge Xian decided he should keep the meeting between the Alliance Leader and Radiant Demon Wing Leader quiet.

'Evil Sister's energy isn't what it was before. Can she resolve the conflict with the Radiant Demon Wing Leader?'

If the Alliance didn't wage sect warfare against Desolate Fortress—

Then that boy would likely become a bridge between the two powers. At least there was no one else from Desolate Fortress better suited.

***

Radiant Demon Wing Leader Jeong Yeon-shin.

The reputation he had built by teaching the Alliance's warriors, his outstanding martial prowess, and his experience as an envoy were all significant.

One had only to look at the Alliance Leader's eyes to see it. He treated the boy like his own grandson.

'If we mean to build friendship with Desolate Fortress, neither I nor Evil Sister can afford to be disliked by the Radiant Demon Wing Leader. I should persuade him carefully.'

It was then.
Sword Star let out a small sigh. A bitter smile soon formed at his lips.

Jeong Yeon-shin answered. His initial displeasure had faded long ago.

Light gathered in the boy's eyes. Even without rotating the Luminous Wheel.

While Sword Star had been evaluating the Radiant Demon Wing Leader, the Radiant Demon Wing Leader had been staring just as intently at Sword Star.

Like a shadow reflected in the eyes of someone gazing into a lake.
Sword Star's questioning response was mild.
Jeong Yeon-shin spoke again.

Blue lightning sparked in the boy's eyes. Sword Star had committed the rudeness of analyzing his martial arts first.

He could hardly complain about gaining insight and testing reactions.

Even if he was a supreme master, Sword Star was aware that the Radiant Demon Wing Leader before him served as Desolate Fortress's envoy.
Sword Star gave a hollow laugh.

[TL Note- Mount Song is where Shaolin Sect is located.]

Jeong Yeon-shin continued slowly.

Sword Star's smile faded.

The boy thought. Now he realized the journey to the Martial Alliance itself had been fortuitous.

He had reached a crucial moment. Sword Star was an inexhaustible waterfall of inspiration.

This had been foreseen when Divine Spear Yue Shou-lin designated Jeong Yeon-shin as suitable for the mission. Unity of essence, energy, and spirit was still far off.

He had to fill the black rank realm with martial technique and the creation of new martial arts.

The new Radiant Demon Wing Leader's words did not stop.

Sword Star Hyeon So-baek's mouth fell slightly open, but the boy paid no mind.

If Sword Star possessed a passion for pursuing the ultimate, Jeong Yeon-shin was born to be enchanted by martial arts he could not imagine.

Was it the pride characteristic of the orthodox righteous path? Sword Star Hyeon So-baek hid nothing.

The boy's eyes were full of interesting things. It was like a restaurant advertising it could cook every delicacy under heaven.

Like the Great River Restaurant in Hangzhou, famous for its excellent food.

The upper dantian's capabilities—growing stronger as death approached—manifested fully.

Inspiration surged like a storm through his mind. It seemed even more so after hearing about Sword Star's background from his senior-like subordinates earlier.

Speculation and knowledge collided with sensation and erupted like fireworks. Everything was inspiration.

He couldn't help but speak. When refined once through words, it returned as polished insight.

He muttered, completely absorbed. These were no longer words directed at anyone.

They were the beginnings of newly born sword techniques.

At this moment, the framework for transforming his swordsmanship was being created.

From nothing more than rumors about Sword Star and the sight of the man standing blankly before him, he had grasped the profound meaning of the sword.

The boy pondered endlessly while observing the supreme master who had reached the stars through his blade.

All that remained was adding flesh and refining through actual combat.

He analyzed a master several levels above while standing in place. Cross-referencing it all against his own reflection.

He was utterly entranced. He hardly appeared human.

He continued thinking with the Luminous Wheel still. Faint blue flames flickered ceaselessly in the boy's eyes.

Zhuge Xian's lips moved blankly. Sword Star Hyeon So-baek also showed an expression of undisguised shock.

The two were orthodox righteous path martial artists. They did not interfere with the boy's comprehension of the sword.

They simply watched, dazed, their faces painted with amazement.

Until the new Radiant Demon Wing Leader, having finally come to his senses, offered a martial salute.

Jeong Yeon-shin clasped his hands and bowed slightly at the waist.

Though a martial salute originally did not involve bowing, it somehow suited him. It was proper etiquette.

Dignity emanated from his gathered black sleeves.

He truly considered it grace. It meant he had gained that much.

Sword Star slowly opened his mouth. He seemed somewhat flustered.
